name: Vitamin D3 Regimen

dosing: Standard maintenance dose is 10,000 IU per day, with some users taking 5,000-20,000 IU depending on blood levels
Loading phase uses 50,000 IU per day for the first 1-2 weeks to rapidly raise serum levels
Bio-Tech D3-50 water-soluble capsules (50,000 IU) are commonly used for the loading phase, taken daily or weekly
preparations: The full regimen (Batch's Anti-Inflammatory Protocol) requires cofactors: Vitamin K2, magnesium, and omega-3 fish oil taken daily alongside D3
Oil-based D3 softgels are standard, while water-soluble forms like Bio-Tech D3-50 offer faster absorption
Magnesium citrate or glycinate is preferred as the cofactor form for better bioavailability
schedule: Begin with a loading dose phase, then transition to a daily maintenance dose and stay on it continuously
Regular blood testing for 25(OH)D3 serum levels is essential, with a target range of 80-100 ng/mL
Monitor serum calcium periodically to avoid hypercalcemia, especially during the loading phase

sideEffects: Generally well tolerated with no side effects at standard doses
Hypercalcemia risk at very high doses without calcium monitoring
May reduce attack severity without fully eliminating attacks
Effectiveness may fade over time for some users
Kidney discomfort possible at very high serum levels, especially with concurrent Topiramate
